title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every forest $H$ there is a function $f$ such that $\chi(G) \leq f(\omega(G))$ for every $H$-free graph $G$.
context_text: The Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture is open in general but has been proved when $H$ is a path. The paper studies $P_5$-free graphs, where $\chi$-boundedness is already established, and focuses on improving the quantitative dependence of $\chi(G)$ on $\omega(G)$.
paper_contribution: Proves the near-polynomial bound $\chi(G) \leq \omega(G)^{\log_2(\omega(G))}$ for $P_5$-free graphs with $\omega(G) \geq 3$, significantly improving the previously best known exponential bound $(5/27)3^{\omega(G)}$ within the Gyárfás-Sumner framework.
notes: The $\chi$-boundedness case for paths (including $P_5$) is already proved; the paper's contribution is an improved quantitative bound, not a new case of the conjecture itself.
paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. IV. A near-polynomial bound for excluding the five-vertex path

title: Conjecture 1.3
statement_text: For every forest $H$, there exists $c > 0$ such that $\chi(G) \leq \omega(G)^c$ for every $H$-free graph $G$.
context_text: This polynomial $\chi$-boundedness conjecture for forest-free graphs is described as beautiful and still open; it has been proved only for a smaller collection of forests and has not been settled for any tree containing $P_5$. Showing it for $H = P_5$ would imply the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture for $P_5$.
paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. IV. A near-polynomial bound for excluding the five-vertex path

title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.6)
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exists $c > 0$ such that $\alpha(G)\omega(G) \geq |G|^c$ for every $H$-free graph $G$.
context_text: The Erdős-Hajnal conjecture is open in general despite great effort; the smallest graph $H$ for which it remains undecided is $P_5$. A polynomial $\chi$-binding function for $P_5$-free graphs would imply the conjecture for $H = P_5$, and the paper's near-polynomial bound is explicitly framed as an attempt to approach this goal.
paper_contribution: Proves the near-polynomial bound $\chi(G) \leq \omega(G)^{\log_2(\omega(G))}$ for $P_5$-free graphs with $\omega(G) \geq 3$, making progress towards establishing the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture for $P_5$, currently the smallest open case.
paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. IV. A near-polynomial bound for excluding the five-vertex path
